calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-16-2024 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-20-2024 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Oct-15-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

PLAINTIFF PETER THEON'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE; AND NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR SANCTIONS
Off Calendar Feb-20-2024 Off Calendar

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; And Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-20-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Bryan A. Reynolds, Esq. BOWMAN AND BROOKE LLP, 310/ 768-3068 Attorney for Defendant
JAGUAR LAND ROVER NORTH AMERICA,
No Other Appearances

Matter is not contested. Defendant's counsel appears out of an abundace of caution and stipulates to JPT Scott Borrowman deciding today's motion. Court and counsel authorize the clerk to sign the JPT Stipulation on their behalf.

No argument is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF PETER THEON'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; And Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions

Motion to compel GRANTED in part and DENIED in part, as follows. RFP 1, 4, 20 - denied. The substantive response identifies what is being produced and the motion does not articulate why this was insufficient. RFP 7 - denied. The objection to overbreadth is sustained because the request, as written, calls for manuals for all manuals not just those related to the make/model at issue. RFP 8, 21 - denied. The objections to overbreadth is sustained based on the clear identification of what Defendant will produce. RFP 9 - denied. The objections are potentially unnecessary but the response clearly states Defendant will produce all repair records in its possession. RFP 12, 13, 17 - denied. The objection based on overbreadth is sustained. The request calls for information about all vehicles, not just those of the same make/model at issue. RFP 18 - denied without prejudice. As written, the request is overbroad and could theoretically call for information about things like personnel records or Board meetings, which would likely be overbroad. However, Plaintiffs are likely entitled to discovery documents sufficient to show its retention policy(ies) for repair record, repair manuals, recall notices, and other categories of documents that are used as exhibits in Lemon Law litigation. The parties are in the better position to meet-and-confer on the types of documents that are potentially in issue and therefore to identify related retention policies. RFP 22 - granted. Defendant must identify those documents it considered in evaluating the request to repurchase this specific document. Any privileged materials should be logged. 23 - denied as substantively duplicative of 22. RFP 24, 25, 26 - denied. The objection to overbreadth is sustained. The request could theoretically call for all documents related to every repurchase request in California (24) or customer complaints (25), including information about other purchasers/plaintiffs. The response offers specific documents it will produce and the motion does not articulate why the response is insufficient. Sanctions
DENIED. Defendant had substantial justification for the responses.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem Scott Borrowman at sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
